description: Removing the artificial `Task.sleep` and streamlining service registration by removing `Task.yield` calls will significantly reduce launch time.

Moving `ModelContext`-dependent service initialization from `MainView.onAppear` to the initial app launch task will perform this work earlier, reducing the time `MainView` is blocked before it can render.

`MainView.onAppear` performs expensive, main-thread work (via `AppBootstrapService`) that blocks UI rendering; moving this logic into `AppRegistry` and executing it earlier in the `App.task` will unblock the UI thread and improve launch time.

`MainView`'s initial render is slow because it builds the view hierarchy for all five tabs at once and initializes all their ViewModels in its `onAppear`. By changing the view's body to a `switch` statement that only renders the active tab, and lazily initializing each tab's ViewModel on its first appearance, we can significantly reduce the initial main-thread work and improve launch time.

Deferring the initialization of the initial `MapViewModel` and `TheOneAPICacheService` in `MainView` until they are actually needed, and simplifying the `SplashView` by removing the computationally intensive `Canvas`-based star field, will reduce main-thread work during the critical launch path.

The app's launch task performs two major synchronous operations sequentially: registering services (`registerServices`) and creating the SwiftData model container (`createModelContainer`). By executing `createModelContainer` on a background thread concurrently with `registerServices` (which must run on the main thread), we can overlap these two expensive operations and reduce the total time spent blocking in the launch task.

The initializers for `LocationDataService`, `EventDataService`, and `PathDataService` perform synchronous, blocking I/O on the main thread; executing these initializations concurrently on background threads using `async let` will reduce the total time spent blocking in `registerServices`.
By concurrently executing `createModelContainer` and the blocking I/O within `registerServices` on background threads, we can parallelize the most expensive startup operations and reduce launch time.

The synchronous SwiftData query `hasCorruptedData()` and potential `deleteAllCharacters()` call within `registerContextDependentServices` are blocking the main thread during launch; moving this work to a detached background task using a separate `ModelContext` will remove this bottleneck from the critical path.

The artificial 400ms sleep is the largest contributor to launch time; replacing it with a `Task.yield()` to allow the UI to render before dismissing the splash will be a major improvement. Additionally, moving a blocking SwiftData query (`hasCorruptedData`) from the main-thread startup path to a detached background task will further reduce launch time.

The primary performance bottlenecks are an artificial 1.8s delay, expensive main-thread work in `MainView.onAppear`, inefficient eager loading of all tabs, and a computationally intensive `Canvas` in `SplashView`. Moving the bootstrap logic into the initial `App.task`, removing the artificial delay, lazily loading tab views with a `switch`, and simplifying the `SplashView` will significantly reduce launch time.

Removing an artificial 1.8s delay, lazily initializing tab views, simplifying the splash screen, and moving a blocking I/O call to a background task will significantly reduce main-thread work during launch.

The `AppBootstrapService.bootstrap()` call in `MainView.onAppear` performs synchronous, main-thread work that blocks the initial rendering of `MainView`; moving this logic into `MiddleEarthApp.task` to run immediately after the `ModelContainer` is created will execute this work before `MainView` is added to the hierarchy, unblocking the UI rendering path and reducing launch time.

The multiple `await Task.yield()` calls in `AppRegistry.registerServices` are adding unnecessary scheduling overhead to the main-thread startup sequence; removing them will reduce launch time by executing all synchronous service initializations in a single, uninterrupted block.

The sequential, main-thread initialization of I/O-bound services (`LocationDataService`, `EventDataService`, etc.) in `AppRegistry.registerServices()` is a significant launch bottleneck; running these initializations concurrently on background threads using `async let` and `Task` will reduce the time spent blocking the main thread.

The current startup process sequentially initializes I/O-bound services on the main thread, and then sequentially creates the SwiftData model container; by parallelizing the I/O-bound service initializations and concurrently creating the model container on background threads, we can significantly reduce the time the main thread is blocked or waiting during the critical startup path.

By concurrently initializing I/O-bound services in the background and simultaneously creating the SwiftData ModelContainer on a background thread, we can parallelize the two most expensive startup operations and significantly reduce the critical path time.

The sequential initialization of I/O-bound services in `registerServices` and the subsequent synchronous creation of the `ModelContainer` on the main thread are major launch bottlenecks; by concurrently initializing the heavy services on background threads within `registerServices` and simultaneously creating the `ModelContainer` on a background thread, we can parallelize the most expensive startup operations and significantly reduce the critical path time.

By parallelizing the I/O-bound service initializations in `AppRegistry.registerServices` and concurrently creating the `ModelContainer` on a background thread, the two largest startup work blocks will run in parallel, significantly reducing the critical path time.

The sequential execution of service registration and ModelContainer creation is a bottleneck; running them concurrently will reduce the critical path time to the duration of the longer of the two tasks.

The sequential initialization of I/O-bound data services on the main actor within `AppRegistry.registerServices` is a major bottleneck; running these initializations concurrently on background threads will significantly reduce the time required for service registration.

The `await Task.yield()` calls in `AppRegistry.registerServices` add unnecessary scheduling overhead during startup; removing them will reduce the total time for service registration by executing all initializations in a single, uninterrupted block on the main thread, thereby reducing the critical path time.

The `AppBootstrapService.bootstrap()` call runs synchronously on the main thread and blocks the `appReady` signal, delaying the initial render of `MainView`; by moving this call into a background task and showing a loading state for non-initial tabs until it completes, we can unblock the critical path and reduce launch time.

The sequential initialization of I/O-bound data services (`LocationDataService`, `EventDataService`, etc.) on the main thread is a major bottleneck in the critical startup path; by initializing these heavy services concurrently on background threads, the time for service registration will be reduced to the duration of the single longest initialization, thus shortening the overall launch time.

The sequential, main-thread initialization of heavy data services is a bottleneck; using `Task.detached` to run these initializations concurrently on background threads, while simultaneously initializing lightweight services on the main thread, will parallelize the work and reduce the total time for service registration.
